steps.text: Keep a record of all safety and health training.
Provide employees with effective information and training on hazardous chemicals in their work area.

preconditions.text: At the time of their initial assignment
Whenever a new chemical hazard the employees have not previously been trained about is introduced into their work area

constraints.text: Chemical-specific information must always be available through labels and safety data sheets.

steps.text: Develop site-specific training programs on the basis of a needs assessment of the hazardous waste site, RCRA/TSDF, or emergency response operation in accordance with 29 CFR 1910.120.
Provide training to each employee who is required to use PPE.
Ensure each affected employee demonstrates an understanding of the PPE training and the ability to use PPE properly before performing work.
Retrain any affected employee who does not possess the required understanding and skill.
Provide effective, comprehensive, and annual training to employees required to use respirators.
Provide the basic information on respirators in Appendix D to employees who wear respirators voluntarily.

preconditions.text: Employee is required by this section to use PPE.
Employee is required to use respirators.

constraints.text: PPE training must cover: when and what PPE is necessary; how to properly don, doff, adjust, and wear it; its limitations; and its proper care, maintenance, useful life, and disposal.
Retraining is required if workplace changes or PPE changes render previous training obsolete, or if the employee's knowledge or use of PPE indicates they have not retained the understanding.
Respirator training must be comprehensive, understandable, and recur annually (or more often if necessary).

steps.text: Evaluate a prospective rescuer’s ability to respond to a rescue summons in a timely manner, considering the hazard(s) identified.
Evaluate a prospective rescue service’s ability, in terms of proficiency with rescue-related tasks and equipment, to function appropriately while rescuing entrants.
Select a rescue team or service from those evaluated that has the capability to reach the victim(s) within an appropriate time frame and is equipped/proficient.
Inform each rescue team or service of the hazards they may confront when called on to perform rescue at the site.
Develop, document, and utilize procedures for the control of potentially hazardous energy when employees are engaged in the activities covered by this section.

preconditions.text: The employer designates rescue and emergency services, pursuant to paragraph (d)(9) of this section.
The servicing or maintenance does not create hazards for other employees.
The employer, in utilizing this exception, has had no accidents involving the unexpected activation or reenergization of the machine or equipment during servicing or maintenance.

constraints.text: When other standards in this part require the use of lockout or tagout, they shall be used and supplemented by the procedural and training requirements of this section.

steps.text: Provide each employee with a training and indoctrination program covering carcinogenic hazards, operations, medical surveillance, decontamination, emergency practices, employee roles, hazard recognition, and first-aid.
Review this section at the employee's first training and indoctrination program and annually thereafter.
Prescribe and post specific emergency procedures.
Familiarize employees with emergency procedures and rehearse them in their application.
Provide training in a program relating to the hazards of vinyl chloride and precautions for its safe use.

preconditions.text: Employee is to be authorized to enter a regulated area.
Employee is engaged in vinyl chloride or polyvinyl chloride operations.

steps.text: Institute a training program and ensure employee participation for each employee who may be exposed to DBCP.
Inform each employee of the required training topics, including Appendix A, DBCP details, respirator use, medical surveillance, and a review of the standard.
Make a copy of this standard and its appendices readily available to all affected employees.
Provide all materials relating to the employee information and training program to the Assistant Secretary and the Director.

preconditions.text: Upon request by the Assistant Secretary and the Director

steps.text: Inform all affected employees of the location of written training materials and make these materials readily available, without cost, to the affected employees.
Provide all training materials relating to the employee training program to the Assistant Secretary and the Director.
Provide employees with information and training on MDA in accordance with 29 CFR 1910.1200(h) at the time of initial assignment and at least annually thereafter.
Provide an explanation of the contents of this section, including Appendices A and B, and indicate to employees where a copy of the standard is available.
Describe the medical surveillance program required under paragraph (m) of this section, and explain the information contained in Appendix C.
Describe the medical removal provision required under paragraph (m) of this section.
Make readily available to all affected employees, without cost, all written materials relating to the employee training program, including a copy of this regulation.
Provide to the Assistant Secretary of Labor and the Director all information and training materials relating to the employee information and training program.

preconditions.text: Upon request by the Assistant Secretary and the Director
Upon request by the Assistant Secretary of Labor and the Director

steps.text: Train employees entering confined/enclosed spaces on characteristics, hazards, health effects, physical signs, PPE, and barriers.
Train entrants to exit the space when evacuation is ordered, an alarm is activated, or danger is perceived.
Provide training before work begins or when operations/duties change.
Certify that the training has been accomplished, including employee's name, certifier's name, and date.
Make the certification available for inspection.

preconditions.text: Employee is assigned to enter a confined space, enclosed space, or other area with dangerous atmospheres.
A change in operations or employee duties presents a new hazard.

constraints.text: Dangerous atmospheres and confined space hazards.

steps.text: Include lead in the program established to comply with the Hazard Communication Standard (HCS) (1910.1200).
Ensure that each employee has access to labels on containers of lead and safety data sheets, and is trained in accordance with the provisions of HCS and paragraph (l).
Institute a training program and ensure employee participation.
Provide the training program as initial training.
Provide the training program at least annually.

preconditions.text: Employee is subject to exposure to lead at or above the action level on any day, or is subject to exposure to lead compounds which may cause skin or eye irritation (e.g., lead arsenate, lead azide).
Prior to the time of job assignment or prior to the start up date for this requirement, whichever comes last.
Employee is subject to lead exposure at or above the action level on any day.

constraints.text: Ensure that at least the following hazards are addressed: Reproductive/developmental toxicity; Central nervous system effects; Kidney effects; Blood effects; and Acute toxicity effects.

steps.text: Select a trained individual or assemble a trained team to audit the process safety management system and program.
Review relevant documentation and process safety information, including the written training program.
Inspect physical facilities and observe actual practices such as safety and health policies, procedures, and work authorization practices.
Interview all levels of plant personnel to determine their knowledge and awareness of safety procedures, duties, rules, and emergency response assignments.
Analyze compliance with the provisions of the standard and other relevant corporate policies using the audit procedure and checklist.
Identify deficiencies and determine where corrective actions or improvements are necessary.

preconditions.text: Audit team members must be chosen for their experience, knowledge, training, and familiarity with the processes and auditing techniques.
The audit must be conducted or led by a person knowledgeable in audit techniques and who is impartial towards the facility or area being audited.

constraints.text: The audit must include an evaluation of the design and effectiveness of the process safety management system and a field inspection of safety and health conditions and practices.

steps.text: Complete training for emergency response employees.
Train employee members of TSD facility emergency response organizations to a level of competence in the recognition of health and safety hazards.
Certify that each covered employee has attended and successfully completed the required training, or certify the employee's competency at least yearly.
Record and maintain the method used to demonstrate competency for certification of training.
Rehearse the emergency response plan regularly as part of the overall training program for site operations.

preconditions.text: Before employees are called upon to perform in real emergencies.

An employer need not train all employees to the degree specified if the workforce is divided such that a sufficient number of trained employees control emergencies, and others have awareness training to summon them.

An employer need not train all employees to the degree specified if advance arrangements are made for an outside fully-trained emergency response team, and first-responding employees have awareness training to call them.

constraints.text: Training must include elements of the emergency response plan, standard operating procedures, personal protective equipment to be worn, and procedures for handling emergency incidents.

TSD training must include methods to minimize risk, safe use of control equipment, selection and use of appropriate PPE, safe operating procedures, coordination techniques, response to overexposure, and recognition of subsequent symptoms.

steps.text: Investigate accidents, injuries, or illnesses occurring on the site or workplace.
Use investigation findings as feedback to determine preventive actions and implement positive steps to prevent recurrence.
Utilize employee suggestions or complaints regarding safety and health issues as a feedback mechanism to improve the program.
Develop, implement, and administer the safety and health program.

preconditions.text: An accident, injury, or illness occurs on the site or workplace.

constraints.text: Professional safety and health personnel (such as Certified Safety Professionals, Board Certified Industrial Hygienists, or Registered Professional Safety Engineers) should be used to develop, implement, and administer the program.

steps.text: Provide respirator training to the employee.
Ensure each employee can demonstrate knowledge of respirator necessity, limitations, emergency use, inspection/checking, maintenance/storage, medical signs, and general requirements.
Administer retraining annually, or when workplace/respirator changes occur, employee knowledge is inadequate, or other safety-critical situations arise.

preconditions.text: Prior to requiring the employee to use a respirator in the workplace.

If a new employee has received training within the last 12 months and can demonstrate knowledge, initial training is not required to be repeated (otherwise, training must be provided no later than 12 months from the previous training date).

constraints.text: The training shall be conducted in a manner that is understandable to the employee.

steps.text: Assign additional personnel to guard against fire while the actual welding, cutting, or heating operation is being performed, and for a sufficient period of time after completion of the work to ensure that no possibility of fire exists.
Instruct assigned personnel as to the specific anticipated fire hazards and how the firefighting equipment provided is to be used.
Take the same precautions on the opposite side of walls, floors, and ceilings as are taken on the side on which the welding is being performed.

preconditions.text: When the welding, cutting, or heating operation is such that normal fire prevention precautions are not sufficient
When welding, cutting, or heating is performed on walls, floors, and ceilings

constraints.text: Direct penetration of sparks or heat transfer may introduce a fire hazard to an adjacent area.

steps.text: Assure that each employee has been provided training in the nature of the hazards associated with work within a controlled decking zone.
Assure that each employee has been provided training in the establishment, access, proper installation techniques and work practices required by 1926.760(c) and 1926.754(e).
Instruct every employee going under air pressure for the first time on how to avoid excessive discomfort.
Perform live-line bare-hand work.
Provide a training program for each employee using ladders and stairways to enable them to recognize and minimize hazards.

preconditions.text: A blaster must be qualified by reason of training, knowledge, or experience in the field of transporting, storing, handling, and use of explosives, and have a working knowledge of State and local laws and regulations.
Employees must be instructed and trained in the live-line bare-hand technique and safety requirements before being permitted to use the technique on energized circuits.

constraints.text: All live-line bare-hand work shall be personally supervised by a person trained and qualified to perform live-line bare-hand work.

steps.text: Include cadmium in the program established to comply with the Hazard Communication Standard (HCS) (1910.1200).
Ensure that each employee has access to labels on containers of cadmium and safety data sheets.
Train employees in accordance with the provisions of HCS and paragraph (m)(4) of this section.
Provide information on at least the following hazards: cancer; lung effects; kidney effects; and acute toxicity effects.
Provide descriptions of the degree of management and employee participation in the field councils' annual reports to the Secretary.
Furnish each agency head with an annual report consolidating the information received as to the participation of the agency’s several field installations in field council activities.
Provide leadership, guidance, and make available necessary equipment, supplies, and staff services to the Field Federal Safety and Health Councils.
Provide consultative and technical services to field councils, including aid in developing and planning programs, and in sponsoring, conducting, or supporting safety and health training courses.
